From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Indian violinist

| Nandini Shankar | |
| --- | --- |

| Background information | |

| Genres | [Hindustani classical music](/wiki/Hindustani_classical_music "Hindustani classical music"), [Fusion](/wiki/Jazz_fusion "Jazz fusion") |

Musical artist

**Nandini Shankar** is an Indian violinist who performs Hindustani classical music and fusion. She is the daughter of [Sangeeta Shankar](/wiki/Sangeeta_Shankar "Sangeeta Shankar")[[1]](#cite_note-1) and granddaughter of [N. Rajam](/wiki/N._Rajam "N. Rajam").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandini_Shankar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Shankar started her training when she was 3 years old and gave her first public performance at 8. She gave her first full-fledged solo performance at the age of 13 years.[[3]](#cite_note-3) She plays violin in the *Gayaki Ang*.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandini_Shankar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Nandini Shanker is grand daughter of Indian violinist [Padma Bhushan](/wiki/Padma_Bhushan "Padma Bhushan") [N. Rajam](/wiki/N._Rajam "N. Rajam") and her mother Dr Sangeeta Shanker is also highly acclaimed violinist. Nandini married the renowned Carnatic music fusion artist, [Mahesh Raghvan](/wiki/Mahesh_Raghvan "Mahesh Raghvan") on 9 May 2021.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandini_Shankar&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Education")]

Nandini Shankar excelled in [academics](/wiki/Academy "Academy"). She completed her [graduation](/wiki/Graduation "Graduation") in commerce, and is a qualified Indian [Chartered Accountant](/wiki/Chartered_accountant "Chartered accountant") (ICAI). She completed her M.A. in Music.

## Performing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nandini_Shankar&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Performing career")]

Shankar performed at [Carnegie Hall](/wiki/Carnegie_Hall "Carnegie Hall")[[4]](#cite_note-carnegie-4) in 2016, [Théâtre de la Ville](/wiki/Th%C3%A9%C3%A2tre_de_la_Ville "Théâtre de la Ville")[[5]](#cite_note-5) in 2021 and [The Esplanade](/wiki/Esplanade_%E2%80%93_Theatres_on_the_Bay "Esplanade – Theatres on the Bay")[[6]](#cite_note-6) in 2023. Countries in which she has performed include the United States,[[4]](#cite_note-carnegie-4) Canada,[[7]](#cite_note-europalia-7) New Zealand,[[8]](#cite_note-nz-8) Switzerland ,[[9]](#cite_note-switzerland-9) the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"),[[10]](#cite_note-uk-10) France, [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), Belgium,[[7]](#cite_note-europalia-7) Venice, Luxembourg,[[11]](#cite_note-11) Greece, [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"),[[12]](#cite_note-meru-12) Hungary, the UAE,[[13]](#cite_note-13) Bahrain,[[14]](#cite_note-14) Bangladesh,[[15]](#cite_note-bengal-15) Malaysia,[[16]](#cite_note-malaysia-16) Sri Lanka, Indonesia[[17]](#cite_note-indonesia-17) and Singapore.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
She has performed at festivals including the [Europalia](/wiki/Europalia "Europalia"),[[7]](#cite_note-europalia-7)
[Sawai Gandharva Bhimsen Festival](/wiki/Sawai_Gandharva_Bhimsen_Festival "Sawai Gandharva Bhimsen Festival"),[[19]](#cite_note-19)
[[20]](#cite_note-20)
[[21]](#cite_note-21)
Yaksha (festival),[[22]](#cite_note-22)
[Saptak Festival of Music](/wiki/Saptak_Festival_of_Music "Saptak Festival of Music"),[[23]](#cite_note-23)
Aarohi for Pancham Nishad,[[24]](#cite_note-24)
[ITC SRA Sangeet Sammelan](/wiki/ITC_SRA_Sangeet_Sammelan "ITC SRA Sangeet Sammelan"),[[25]](#cite_note-itc-25)
MERU in Netherlands,[[12]](#cite_note-meru-12)
Jaya Smriti organised by [Hema Malini](/wiki/Hema_Malini "Hema Malini"),[[26]](#cite_note-26)
Temple of Fine Arts,[[16]](#cite_note-malaysia-16)
Bhilwara Sur Sangam,[[27]](#cite_note-27) The Association of Performing Arts of India,[[28]](#cite_note-28)
[T. N. Krishnan](/wiki/T._N._Krishnan "T. N. Krishnan") Foundation,[[29]](#cite_note-29)
Delhi International Arts Festival,[[30]](#cite_note-30)
Bengal Music Foundation,[[15]](#cite_note-bengal-15)
and [Dover Lane Music Conference](/wiki/Dover_Lane_Music_Conference "Dover Lane Music Conference").[[31]](#cite_note-31) She has been telecast in [Idea Jalsa](/wiki/Idea_Jalsa "Idea Jalsa")[[32]](#cite_note-32) and made a music video with her sister [Ragini Shankar](/wiki/Ragini_Shankar "Ragini Shankar").[[33]](#cite_note-33)

Nandini Shankar and her sister [Ragini](/wiki/Ragini_Shankar "Ragini Shankar") have been signed by Decca Records US, a part of Universal Music Group for a full-length music album for their musical ensemble 'Taraana’.[[34]](#cite_note-34) She regularly releases singles with her husband [Mahesh Raghvan](/wiki/Mahesh_Raghvan "Mahesh Raghvan"). She is a part of Sakhi, India's first all-girl Indian classical musical band, formed by [Kaushiki Chakraborty](/wiki/Kaushiki_Chakraborty "Kaushiki Chakraborty").[[35]](#cite_note-35) She is also a part of 'inStrings', a fusion band combining various genres from across the world.

She resides in Mumbai.
